title: Inspect retained report history and status
description: Read one immutable snapshot, list bounded metadata-first history, and inspect metadata-only store status without changing retained content. Goal trace: G4, G6, and G7.
rationale: The owner and recovery tooling need deterministic history visibility without dumping every financial report by default.
story_want: find retained reports and inspect local report-store health
story_so_that: history and recovery state remain manageable without over-disclosure
acceptance_criteria: Given a known retained identity, when Get is requested, then the exact immutable Report Snapshot and its complete provenance are returned.
Given an unknown retained identity, when Get is requested, then Not Found is returned without other report metadata.
Given a deleted retained identity, when Get is requested, then Deleted and metadata-only Deletion Event evidence are returned without report content.

Given a bounded List request, when it succeeds, then stable report identity, predecessor identity, Budget Period, trusted creation time, actor, policy versions, content fingerprint, and deletion state are returned without financial payload.
Given retained history, when List ordering is inspected, then it is trusted creation time descending followed by stable report identity descending.
Given a List request over the released count or output limit, when it is validated, then resource failure is returned rather than silent truncation.

Given Status, when it is requested, then store version, integrity state, retained count, deleted count, latest successful backup/verification evidence, and supported policy versions are returned without amounts, merchant text, report bodies, or private paths.
Given Get, List, or Status success or failure, when retained history is inspected afterwards, then no Report Snapshot, Deletion Event, or idempotency outcome changed.
security_criteria: List and Status are metadata-only; report payload requires an explicit Get for one stable identity.
